stories.title: CPSC Recall — Vevor Baby Swings Pose Suffocation/Death Risk to Infants

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
RECALL: Vevor baby swings pulled due to deadly suffocation risk. (~1,020 units)

They were marketed for infant sleep — but violate the Safe Sleep for Babies Act.

Stop use now. Call 855-599-6320 for a refund.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
If you have a Vevor baby swing (models BB501K, BB702A, or BB005K):

Stop use. Destroy the swing cover. Mark the base "RECALLED."

Contact Sanven Technology at 855-599-6320 for a full refund.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
**Window:** 8:00 AM MT

Important safety alert for families with infants.

The U.S. Consumer Product Safety Commission has announced the recall of approximately 1,020 Vevor baby swings sold by Sanven Technology. These swings were marketed for infant sleep — but they have an incline angle greater than 10 degrees, which violates the Safe Sleep for Babies Act and poses a serious suffocation risk. When an infant sleeps on an inclined surface, their head can tip forward and block their airway. This is a documented cause of infant deaths.

The recalled models are the BB501K (white/gray swing, white base), BB702A (white/gray swing, gray base), and BB005K (black/gray swing, black base). They were sold on Vevor.com and Amazon.com from January 2025 through August 2025, priced between $65 and $80.

What to do right now:
1. Stop using the swing immediately.
2. Destroy the swing cover so no one else can use it.
3. Mark the base with "RECALLED."
4. Take photos of both the cover and the base.
5. Call Sanven Technology at 855-599-6320 (Monday–Friday, 9 AM–5 PM PT) for a full refund.

Please share this post. Safe sleep is flat, firm, and on their back — always.
facebook.image_caption:
**Window:** 8:00 AM MT
RECALL: Vevor baby swings (models BB501K, BB702A, BB005K) pose a suffocation risk and violate the Safe Sleep for Babies Act. Stop use now. Call 855-599-6320 for a full refund. ⚠️

article.headline: CPSC Recall: Vevor Baby Swings Recalled for Suffocation Risk — What COS Families Need to Know

stories.title: Garden of the Gods Art Festival — May 16–17, Free for Kids Under 12

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
COS families: Garden of the Gods Art Festival is May 16–17 at Rock Ledge Ranch. 🎨

150 artists. Live music. Food trucks. Kids under 12 get in free.

$10 adults, $8 seniors/military.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting fanrumor.com

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
Free shuttle from Coronado High School to the Garden of the Gods Art Festival May 16–17.

Runs 9:30 AM–5 PM both days. Dogs on leash welcome too.

It's a great Colorado Springs weekend for families.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
**Window:** 12:00 PM MT

A beautiful way to spend a weekend with your family is coming up fast.

The 3rd Annual Garden of the Gods Art Festival runs Saturday and Sunday, May 16–17, at Rock Ledge Ranch Historic Site in Colorado Springs. Hours are 10 AM–5 PM both days. Against the backdrop of Pikes Peak and the iconic red rock formations, 150 juried fine artists and craftsmen will be displaying and selling pottery, jewelry, textiles, paintings, sculpture, and glass art. There's live music, kids activities, and food trucks too.

The practical details families want to know:
- Kids under 12 get in free
- Adults: $10
- Seniors 65+ and military (with ID): $8
- Rock Ledge Ranch members: free
- Free shuttle runs from Coronado High School, 9:30 AM–5 PM both days
- Well-behaved dogs on leash are welcome

This is one of the most scenic outdoor events of the year in COS, and with the free shuttle and free admission for kids, it's a very accessible outing. The festival is May 16–17, so mark your calendars.

Who else is putting this on their May calendar?
facebook.image_caption:
**Window:** 12:00 PM MT
Garden of the Gods Art Festival: May 16–17 at Rock Ledge Ranch. 150 artists. Kids under 12 free. Free shuttle from Coronado High School. What a weekend. 🎨

article.headline: Garden of the Gods Art Festival May 16–17: Kids Under 12 Get In Free at This Colorado Springs Weekend Event

stories.title: CPSC Recall — Bellabu Bear Children's Robes Violate Flammability Standards

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
RECALL: Bellabu Bear bamboo sherpa robes (PAW Patrol, Minecraft, Garfield, etc.) don't meet federal flammability standards.

Sold at Saks Fifth Avenue + online. ~$60.

Call 888-703-7752 for a full refund.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
**Window:** 10:00 AM MT

Check your kids' closets this week.

Bellabu Bear has recalled its line of bamboo sherpa children's robes because they don't meet federal flammability standards for children's sleepwear. That's a burn hazard — and these robes were popular gifts, often bought at Saks Fifth Avenue or online for around $60.

The recalled robes come in a wide range of licensed character designs: PAW Patrol, Minecraft, Garfield, Minions, Batman, Justice League, Wonder Woman, Blaze and the Monster Machines, Trolls, Dora, and others, including non-licensed patterns like nutcracker, hearts, Santa, stripes, bows, and dragons. They're sold in children's sizes S/M and M/L, with "Bellabu Bear," "BB-NW12109," and the size printed on the neck label.

The robes were sold from January 2024 through July 2025.

If you have one of these robes:
- Stop letting your child use it right away
- Contact Bellabu Bear at 888-703-7752 (Monday–Friday, 9 AM–4 PM PT)
- Or email help@bellabubear.com
- Or visit bellabubear.com and click "Recall" at the bottom of the page
- You'll receive a full refund or store credit, plus a prepaid return label

This is the kind of recall that's easy to miss because the robes look perfectly fine. Please share this — especially if anyone you know got one of these as a gift.
facebook.image_caption:
**Window:** 10:00 AM MT
Bellabu Bear bamboo sherpa robes recalled for burn hazard. PAW Patrol, Minecraft, Garfield, and other character designs. Check labels: "Bellabu Bear" + "BB-NW12109." Call 888-703-7752 for a refund. ⚠️

stories.title: Summer Swim Safety — What COS Families Need to Know Before the Season Opens

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
Memorial Day is 3 weeks away. Water safety season starts now.

Drowning is the No. 1 cause of death in kids ages 1–4.

AAP: swim lessons can start at age 1. Eyes on kids near water, always.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting fanrumor.com

facebook.long_form:
**Window:** 5:00 PM MT

Memorial Day weekend is less than three weeks away — and that means water season in Colorado Springs is almost here.

This is the time to get ahead of swim safety, not the first day of summer. Drowning is the leading cause of unintentional death among children ages 1–4 in the United States. It happens quickly and silently — it doesn't look like it does in movies. An adult can be nearby and not notice until it's too late.

The American Academy of Pediatrics recommends these layers of protection:

1. Swim lessons starting at age 1. Many parents wait until 3 or 4, but the AAP says swim readiness for many children starts earlier than that.
2. Four-sided pool fencing. If you have a backyard pool, it needs a 4-sided fence with a self-closing, self-latching gate — not just a fence on three sides.
3. "Touch supervision." For young children, an adult should be within arm's length at all times in or near water. Phones down.
4. Life jackets for open water. Any time kids are near natural water — lakes, rivers, reservoirs — they should be in a properly-fitted USCG-approved life jacket.

In Colorado Springs, the YMCA of the Pikes Peak Region offers swim programs for all ages. COS Parks and Recreation outdoor pools open for the season later this month. Cheyenne Mountain State Park's Sage Brush Lake opens for recreational use as weather warms.

What's your water safety plan for this summer?
facebook.image_caption:
**Window:** 5:00 PM MT
Drowning is the No. 1 cause of unintentional death in kids ages 1–4. The YMCA and AAP say: get swim lessons early, use 4-sided pool fencing, and never look away. Water season starts soon in COS. 💧

article.headline: Summer Swim Safety: What Colorado Springs Families Need to Know Before Memorial Day Weekend

stories.title: D11 Reports Enrollment Increase for First Time in 12 Years

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Colorado Springs School District 11 just reported its first enrollment increase in 12 years — 86 more students than last year.

That can bring in nearly $1 million in additional state and federal funding for D11.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
**Window:** 8:00 PM MT

Here's some genuinely good news about one of Colorado Springs' school districts.

Colorado Springs School District 11 has reported an enrollment increase for the first time in 12 years. The district counted 86 more students enrolled than at the same point last year. It might not sound like a lot — but in school funding, it matters significantly. Each additional student brings in per-pupil funding from the state and federal government, meaning D11 could receive nearly $1 million in additional dollars as a result of this turnaround.

D11 serves central Colorado Springs and has a wide range of school options, including magnet schools, choice enrollment, and specialized programs. Nearly 3,000 students who live within D11 boundaries currently attend other districts like D20 and D49. Families who've been exploring their options for the 2026–27 school year can find enrollment and school choice information at d11.org.

After more than a decade of declining enrollment, this is a meaningful data point. It suggests families are choosing to stay — or come back — to D11 schools.

Were you surprised by this news? Let us know in the comments.
facebook.image_caption:
**Window:** 8:00 PM MT
D11 enrollment is up for the first time in 12 years — 86 more students, and nearly $1 million in additional funding. Good news for Colorado Springs families who call D11 home. 📚

article.headline: D11 Reports Enrollment Increase for the First Time in 12 Years — What It Means for Colorado Springs Families
